channel.title: Deed on Berrytree.org
channel.description: Recent content in Deed on Berrytree.org

item.title: Chapter 1: Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys & Kemps
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Preface | Table of Contents | Chapter 2: Robert Berry →

Chapter 1#

Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys & Kemps#

I have been researching the Robert Berry family from Orange County since 1997. When I first started researching, I found that the Census forms from 1790 to 1840, only listed the head of household’s given and surnames. Sometimes they would be listed alphabetically, which was not totally useless but not very helpful either. When the families were listed in the order of farms and plantation physical location, the information became more valuable. I would search the census forms from the very early years and become familiar with as many of the surnames located near one of my known ancestors as possible. Soon I learned that a cluster of surnames in one county would often vary greatly from the surnames listed in the very next county. Since the population was small, clusters of surnames in a close proximity were often related to each other. As our ancestors relocated from one state to another, the pattern was repeated over and over. When I located a family surname who seemed to be a relative from a previous community, I started looking at the surnames of his neighbors. I always paid particular attention to these very close neighbors. This is a very important tool; since there were really not very many people in these early settlements, many of them migrated there together. Many of them were friends and a lot of them were relatives and children who had known each other their entire lives. They had grown up together and when the time came to relocate, they chose to go together.

item.title: John Robert Berry
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. John Robert Berry
John Robert Berry

Generation 5 John Robert Berry

Born
September 25, 1842, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
October 10, 1917, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
Elizabeth Frances Bowling — married November 30, 1865
Parents
Thomas Person Berry and Sarah Lunsford, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline
Direct Berry Line
  1. Robert and Mary Williamson Berry — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  2. Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry — Orange County, North Carolina
  3. William Berry and Hannah Cate — Orange County, NC
  4. Thomas Person Berry and Sarah Lunsford — Orange County, NC
  5. John Robert Berry and Elizabeth Frances Bowling — Orange County, NC

Early Life#

John Robert Berry was born on September 25, 1842, in Orange County, North Carolina. He was the third son of Thomas Person and Sarah Lunsford Berry. There is not a lot known about the period between Thomas Person Berry and Sarah Lunsford’s marriage on September 28, 1831, and the birth of their first son in 1836.

item.title: Chapter 2: Robert Berry 1729–1814
item.description:
Our Berrys in Frontier America by Benjamin Berry Henderson Sr.

← Chapter 1: Williamsons, Fosters, Berrys, Kemps | Table of Contents | Chapter 3: Mary Berry →

Chapter 2#

Robert Berry 1729–1814#

Robert Berry OC came to a part of Granville County, North Carolina, which was soon to become Orange County, NC. We know that he built a small cabin in the northeast corner of his staked out land. This cabin was documented in Robert Berry’s OC Entry for his Land Grant. Thomas Bradford owned the land just north of Robert Berry’s OC claim.

Died

Married
Elizabeth Cate — married c. 1759, Orange County, NC
Parents
Robert Berry and Mary Williamson, Princess Anne County, VA
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Robert Berry#

Robert Berry was the son of Robert Berry and Mary Williamson of Princess Anne County, Virginia, and he came to Orange County, North Carolina, in 1751.

item.description:

Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 3 (by marriage) Hannah Cate Berry

Born
1770, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
1858, Orange County, North Carolina
Married
William Berry — married August 12, 1799, Orange County, NC
Parents
William and Elizabeth Smith (Messersmith) Cate
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Hannah Cate Berry#

Hannah Cate was born in 1770 and she died in 1858 in Orange County, NC (1810 and 1850 Orange County Censuses; William Cate’s will, dated November 10, 1775, confirms children). Hannah Cate was the daughter of William and Elizabeth Smith (Messersmith) Cate.

item.title: Robert Berry (PAC)
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Robert Berry (PAC)
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina

Generation 1 Robert Berry (PAC)

Born
Unknown — probably born in England
Died
c. 1730, Princess Anne County, Virginia
Married
Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Parents
Unknown — most likely born in England
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Robert Berry (PAC)#

Robert Berry (PAC) of South Norfolk County, Virginia, married Mary Williamson and established a home in Princess Anne County, Virginia, which is now known as Virginia Beach, Virginia. Robert and Mary had two sons. The oldest was Richard Berry and the baby was Robert Berry “Orange County, NC” (OC) who was born about 1729. Robert Berry (PAC) wrote his will in 1730 and died shortly after Robert Berry (Orange County, NC) was born.

item.title: Sarah Cate Berry Pigg
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Sarah Cate Berry Pigg
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3Thomas Berry × Sarah Cate — Orange County, NC → Wayne County, TN

Generation 3 (by marriage) Sarah Cate Berry Pigg

Born
December 27, 1782, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
March 7, 1875, Wayne County, Tennessee
Married
1st: Thomas Berry — married August 11, 1800, Orange County, NC · 2nd: John Pigg — married December 11, 1815, Williamson County, TN
Parents
William Cate and Elizabeth Messersmith Cate
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Sarah Cate#

Sarah Cate was the daughter of William and Elizabeth Messersmith Cate and was born in North Carolina on December 27, 1782. Nothing is known about Sarah’s early life except that she had two brothers and two sisters. The brothers were John Cate and Benjamin Cate and her two sisters were Charity and Hannah Cate.

item.title: William Clarence Berry NC, ALA, TX
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. William Clarence Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 4William Clarence Berry × Sally Bowles / Massa Ann Tompkins — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, AL → Burnet County, TX

Generation 4 William Clarence Berry

Born
October 17, 1803, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
September 8, 1878, Burnet County, Texas
Married
1st: Sally Bowles (married November 20, 1826; died 1833) — 2nd: Massa Ann Tompkins (married 1836)
Parents
William Berry and Hannah Cate Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

William Clarence Berry, son of William and Hannah Cate Berry#

William Clarence Berry was born on October 17th 1803 in Orange County, North Carolina and died on 08 September 1878 in Burnet County Texas. He and his two brothers were listed on Hannah Cate Berry’s 1810 census. The family was living in the old Patrick Rutherford plantation house which Robert Berry bought in 1787. They may have lived there for many years but Hannah did not list in the 1820 Orange County, NC, census.

item.title: Aldridge Berry Farquhar Waggoner Connection
item.description:
The Aldridge, Berry, Farquhar & Waggoner Connection

Four families intermarried across multiple generations from Orange County, NC, to Fayette County, Alabama, and Burnet County, Texas.

There are several marriages in different Berry lines involving these four families.

From Henry Waggoners 1784 Will (Orange County NC Deed Book A Page 231) Henry and Ketriana Waggoner’s Children:

item.title: Berry Wills and Deeds from PAC, 1691–1755
item.description:
Berry Wills & Deeds — Princess Anne County, Virginia

12 Berry family entries from deed books 1–7, 1691–1755. Abstracted by Anne Mailing.

Only 12 listings were found for Berry wills and deeds in Princess Anne County, Virginia, and only three Berrys appeared in the Virginia 1704 quit rent rolls. In the list below, wd = will dated, wp = will probated.

item.title: Deed Book Page 331 Land Purchase in Person County NC
item.description:
Deed Book Page 331

Robert Berry Jr.'s land purchase in Person County, North Carolina.

This deed book entry records Robert Berry Jr.’s land purchase in Person County, North Carolina.

item.title: Deed Book Page 421 Robert Berry Jr - land sold
item.description:
Deed Book Page 421

Robert Berry Jr.'s land sale record, Person County, North Carolina.

This deed book entry records Robert Berry Jr.’s land sale in Person County, North Carolina.

item.title: Georgia Land Document
item.description:
David J. Berry in Georgia

Land transactions and migration from Orange County, NC, through Fayette, Henry, Cobb, and Milton Counties, Georgia.

Ancestors: Generation 1 — Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry Generation 2 — David J. Berry and Mary Blalock

Back to David J. Berry Page →

item.title: James Berry Tenn
item.description:
James Berry, son of Joshua Berry and Nancy Ellison Berry#

  • Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
  • Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
  • Gen 3 Joshua Berry × Nancy Ellison — Orange County, North Carolina

James Berry was born in 1800 in Orange County, North Carolina. James Berry and Mary Parker were married in North Carolina about 1829. Their first three daughters were born in North Carolina. The oldest daughter, Nancy, was born in 1830. The youngest daughter, Delila, was born in North Carolina in 1836 and was 14 years old in the 1850 Smith County, Tennessee census. The oldest child born in Tennessee was, Martha, who was 11 years old in 1850. This means James and Mary were in North Carolina in 1836 and in Tennessee in 1839. I have no absolute proof that the James Berry on this page is related to us but all the facts indicate he was..

Robert Berry’s Deed to Joshua Berry with James Berry as a witness. Dated February 24 1811 (Orange County Deed Book 14 Page 57)

item.title: Page Updates
item.description:
Site Update History

A running log of new pages, content additions, and research updates. Latest entries are at the top.

If you have pictures or information about your family line, we’d love to hear from you through our Contact page.

  • 4/04/2026 Created James Thomas Wilson page and updated Pattie Berry Wilson page

item.title: Proof Page
item.description:
Proof of the Berry-Williamson Connection

Evidence linking Robert Berry of Orange County, NC, to Princess Anne County, Virginia, through the Williamson and Kempe families.

This page assembles the documentary evidence connecting Robert Berry of Orange County, NC, to Princess Anne County, Virginia, through shared surnames, tax lists, and land records.

item.title: Relatives Found in PAC Deed Book
item.description:
Relatives Found in PAC Deed Book

Berry, Kempe, Williamson, and Carraway family entries from Princess Anne County deed books.

Names of Relatives

George and Mary Hutchinson Kempe’s Children;

  1. Ann Kempe
  2. Mary Kempe
  3. Thomas Kempe married Sally Thorowgood 17 Jul 1779 (PAC)
  4. Elizabeth Kempe
  5. Sarah Kempe
  6. James Kempe married Frances [Mrs] Shepherd 23 Jun 1762 (PAC)
  7. John Kempe

Richard Williamson married Mary Kempe- Their Children;

item.title: Robert Berry Joshua's Son NC
item.description:
Robert Berry, Joshua's Son

Son of Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry, Orange County, North Carolina. Married Sally Cates.

Robert Berry, son of Joshua Berry and Nancy Ellison Berry#

Ancestors#

  1. Generation Robert and Mary Williamson Berry: Princess Anne County, Virginia.
  2. Generation Robert and Elizabeth Cate Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.
  3. Generation Joshua and Nancy Ellison Berry: Orange County, North Carolina.

ROBERT BERRY was born in 1800 in Orange County North Carolina. Robert Berry and Sally Cates were married in Person County North Carolina circa 1814. There is a great deal that we did not know about this family. We know that they did live in Orange County for their entire lives or at least until 1850. Sally Berry age 52 (born 1798) listed as head of household in 1850 with her two daughters Rachel Berry age 11 (born 1839) and Sarah Berry age 14 (born 1836).

item.title: Robert C. Berry NC Ala
item.description:
1. Home
  2. Ancestors
  3. Family Lines
  4. Robert C. Berry
Gen 1 Robert Berry × Mary Williamson — Princess Anne County, Virginia
Gen 2 Robert Berry × Elizabeth Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 3William Berry × Hannah Cate — Orange County, North Carolina
Gen 4Robert C. Berry × Sarah Chappell — Orange County, NC → Fayette County, Alabama

Generation 4 Robert C. Berry

Born
April 14, 1805, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
Fayette County (Berry), Alabama
Married
Sarah Chappell (married c. 1839, Fayette County, Alabama)
Parents
William Berry and Hannah Cate Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

Son of William and Hannah Cate Berry#

Robert C. Berry was born on April 14th 1805 in Orange County, North Carolina. Robert C. did not marry in Orange County and was about 29 or 30 years old when he left the state. Since he was single and didn’t own property there are no official records naming him. He did however witness the sale of property on September 19th 1833 that his two brothers sold.

Robert C. Berry was born on April 14th 1805 in Orange County, North Carolina. Robert C. did not marry in Orange County and was about 29 or 30 years old when he left the state. Since he was single and didn’t own property there are no official records naming him. He did however witness the sale of property on September 19th 1833 that his two brothers sold.

Born
September 1, 1919, Orange County, North Carolina
Died
August 18, 2007
Married
Mary Elizabeth Barbee (born January 13, 1928; died January 25, 2014)
Parents
Wiley P. Berry Sr. and Ida Ann Wilson Berry, Orange County, NC
Explore in the Berry Family Timeline

The Story of Wiley P. Berry Jr.#

The picture below is of the Wiley P. Berry Family and was taken around 1925. Their second oldest son, Thomas Young Berry was born on May 11 1903 and died on June 8 1925. He may be the one taking the picture or he may have already died. L-R Sally Malena, Mary Elizabeth, Woodrow Wilson, William Eugene, Wiley Harold, Mother: Ida Ann Wilson Berry, Wiley P. Berry Jr, and Father: Wiley P. Berry Sr.

item.title: William Clarence Berry NC, ALA, TX
item.description:
William Clarence Berry (1803–1877)

Son of William and Hannah Cate Berry, from Orange County NC to Fayette County Alabama and Burnet County Texas.

William Clarence Berry, son of William and Hannah Cate Berry Berry#

ANCESTORS

Generation 1 Robert and Mary Williamson Berry VA

item.title: Williamson Deeds and Wills 1691–1755, Princess Anne County
item.description:
Williamson Family Deeds & Wills — Princess Anne County, Virginia

Over 50 abstracts from deed books 1–7 covering the Williamson family from 1691 to 1755. Abstracted by Anne Mailing.

  • The first date shown in the will is the date it was written
  • The second date is when it was probated ** 1DB193 Page 13**

25 Jul 1698 / 7 Sep: 1698 **Bartholomew WILLIAMSON **- son Charles, son James land bought of brother-in-law William CHRISTOPHER, wife Martha daughter Sarah wife of Thomas HARRYSON, daughter of Mary Widow of Francis Bond, daughter Elizabeth wife of Thomas SPRATT, daughter Martha grandson Bartholomew son of Francis BOND. Ex: wife and Charles. Overseers: Benoni BOROUGH, Edward MOSLEY, Sr., brother Richard. Wit: Edward MOSLEY, Sr., William MOSLEY Sr., Thomas HARRISON, Francis MOSLEY.
